registration.type: a Non-Banking Financial Company (Base Layer) registered with the

content: provides unsecured payday loans, personal loans, EMI-based loans, Loan Against Property (LAP), and corporate/business loans. The Company
s primary objective is to maintain healthy asset quality while ensuring recovery actions are cost-effective, compliant, transparent and consistent with RBI regulations.

purposeOfPolicy.start: This Policy has been formulated to:
items: Provide a standardised, transparent, and fair mechanism for compromise settlements, loan settlements, and technical write-offs.
Ensure full compliance with the RBI
Define detailed processes, documentation, approval authorities, due diligence steps, internal controls, and reporting requirements.
Establish clear principles for determining settlement amounts, security valuation, hardship verification, and recovery options.
Prevent misuse, arbitrary waivers, conflicts of interest, and coercive recovery practices.
Ensure settlements maximize recoveries at minimum cost.
provisioning norms are followed while writing off assets.

content: A negotiated agreement with a borrower to fully settle the Company
s claims for a mutually agreed amount which is less than total dues, resulting in a sacrifice or waiver by the Company. (As per RBI definition).
A structured or lump-sum settlement for stressed or NPA accounts, usually involving upfront payment and closure within a short time frame.
A lump-sum repayment made by the borrower in exchange for waiver of remaining dues.
An NPA loan where the borrower still legally owes the amount but the Company removes it from its books only for accounting purposes, without waiving the right to recover.

content.intro: A loan may be considered for settlement when:
points: Account already written-off but borrower is now reachable.
Collateral value (for secured loans) insufficient to cover outstanding dues.
Litigation cost is expected to exceed realizable recovery.
points2: High roll-rate and low likelihood of recovery
points3: Borrower unable to meet EMI obligations
Written-off accounts with potential for partial recovery
points4: Fresh valuation showing lower realizable value
points5: Business closure / insolvency / severe cash-flow deterioration
Market value of collateral less than outstanding

points: Technical write-off does NOT reduce borrower liability. The write-off shall be effected only in the books of accounts of the Company.
Technical write-off accounts must continue to be actively pursued for recovery.
Partial technical write-offs must ensure provisioning = 100% of gross exposure as required by RBI.
Re-aging of accounts is not allowed.
Any waiver must follow compromise settlement norms, not technical write-off norms.
Recovery efforts, including legal action, collection follow-ups, or settlement negotiations, may continue even after the loan is written off in the books.

freshLoanEligibility.description: The Company does not provide fresh loans to customers whose accounts were settled under compromise or classified as technical write-off. Exceptions may be granted on specific customer request, subject to proper verification of the case, assessment of creditworthiness, and approval by the competent authority.
freshLoanEligibility.coolingPeriod: A general cooling period of 12-18 months shall be observed.

settlement.title: In Case of Settlement
points: Waiver must be booked as a loss.
Recovery posted to loan account accordingly.
Written-off assets must be reported separately in financial statements.

writeOff.title: In Case of Write-off
points: Upon write-off, the outstanding principal and accrued interest (if any) shall be removed from the books by debiting the Loan Loss Provision Account.
Any subsequent recoveries from written-off accounts shall be recognized as Other Income / Recovery from Written-Off Accounts in the period in which they are realized.
Interim review if RBI issues new directions.
All amendments recorded and notified internally.

factors.title: 6.2 Factors Influencing Settlement Calculation
points: Previous recovery performance in similar cases

internalMonitoring.title: 11.1 Internal Monitoring
points: Monthly MIS of settlements and write-offs
High-risk accounts flagged to senior management

auditRequirements.title: 11.2 Audit Requirements
points: Internal audit to verify documentation, approvals & compliance

delegationOfAuthority.note: Settlement approvals must be one level higher than loan sanctioning authority.
delegationOfAuthority.footerNote: All approvals are based on Due Past Days (DPD) of the customer.

steps.title: Step 1: Borrower Communication
points: Borrower request received in writing, email, app request, or call recorded.
Borrower should be informed about credit bureau impact.

steps.title: Step 3: Recovery Assessment
steps.intro: A detailed assessment note must include:
points: Expected loss estimate vs. settlement recovery

steps.title: Step 6: Payment
points: Payment must be completed within settlement validity period.
